description: Create the Zapier webhook API route and lead submission utility that all forms will use. This includes the server-side API endpoint that receives lead data, enriches it with metadata (form source, page URL, timestamp, UTM params, lead score), and forwards to Zapier webhook. Also create a client-side utility function that forms call to submit lead data.
steps: Create app/api/lead-capture/route.ts as a POST endpoint following the pattern in app/api/email-results/route.ts. It should: (1) Accept JSON body with fields: email (required), name (optional), formSource (string identifying which form), pageUrl (string), timestamp (ISO string), utmParams (object with utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_content, utm_term), and calculatorContext (optional object with calculatorName, resultSummary for calculator-gate form). (2) Validate email is present and contains @. (3) Compute a lead_score (0-100) based on: form source (+30 for calculator-gate since high intent, +20 for exit-intent since engaged enough to get lead magnet, +15 for newsletter since mid-intent, +10 for blog-sidebar and bottombar); whether name was provided (+15); whether UTM params present (+10 for paid campaigns utm_medium=cpc/paid); calculator context present (+20). Cap at 100. (4) Determine a lead_segment string: 'calculator_power_user' if calculatorContext present, 'content_reader' if blog-sidebar, 'newsletter_subscriber' if newsletter or bottombar, 'exit_intent_capture' if exit-intent. (5) Build the payload to forward to Zapier: { email, name, form_source, page_url, timestamp, utm_params, lead_score, lead_segment, calculator_context, submitted_at: new Date().toISOString() }. (6) Fire-and-forget POST to process.env.ZAPIER_WEBHOOK_URL (do not await response or block user). If env var is not set, just log to console. (7) Return { success: true, lead_score } to the client.

Create lib/lead-capture-api.ts as a client-side utility function submitLeadCapture({ email, name, formSource, pageUrl, utmParams, calculatorContext }) that: (1) Reads UTM params from window.location.search if not provided. (2) Calls fetch('/api/lead-capture', { method: 'POST', ... }) with the data. (3) Is fire-and-forget from the caller's perspective (returns a Promise but callers don't need to await it for UX). (4) Includes error handling that logs but never throws to the user.
Add ZAPIER_WEBHOOK_URL= to .env.example with a comment explaining it's the Zapier Catch Hook URL for lead capture automation.
Add NEXT_PUBLIC_LEAD_CAPTURE_ENABLED=true to .env.example with a comment explaining it enables/disables the Zapier webhook integration (forms still work locally without it).
acceptance_criteria: app/api/lead-capture/route.ts exists and exports a POST handler
The POST handler validates email, computes lead_score, determines lead_segment, and forwards to ZAPIER_WEBHOOK_URL
The forward to Zapier is fire-and-forget (uses fetch without awaiting or with a .catch that doesn't propagate)
lib/lead-capture-api.ts exports a submitLeadCapture function that posts to /api/lead-capture
The client utility parses UTM params from the URL automatically
verification: Run pnpm build and confirm no TypeScript or build errors
Run pnpm lint and confirm no linting errors
